author: potuz
content: Can I get a quick rundown of what do clients do when receiving data columns for a block you haven't yet seen? this is killing us on devnets

author: tersec
content: put it in something we call a "column quarantine" and wait to correlate it with a block and payload

author: potuz
content: are you not worried that you get spammed by these?

author: tersec
content: yes, but it's not clear there's a better alternative

author: tersec
content: it's at least limited/filtered by custody

author: potuz
content: thanks... we're also queing them but for some reason we're getting killed, still diagnosing this

author: mehdi.aouadi
content: I have a question regarding the POST `/eth/v1/beacon/execution_payload_bids` API: how do other clients respond when the submitted bid should have been ignored based on the gossip rules (the matching proposer preferences not seen yet for example)? Do you apply the p2p gossip rules before responding? Because Teku responds with a 400 (we run the same gossip rules for validation) and it looks like it's not the case for other clients based on some devnet-7 analysis. I'm wondering if we should clarify the beacon-api spec (respond with 202 in that case for example)

author: pawandhananjay
content: lighthouse also queues them. max 256 columns and reprocess when the block arrives
